raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "Prescribes a tapering protocol as a committed practitioner behavior for administering suggestions until self-sustaining change occurs",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "Suggestions lose potency without periodic renewal",
    "Progress is reliably observable and permits systematic lengthening of intervals",
    "Complete cure is marked by the point at which external suggestions become unnecessary",
    "The same spacing rule applies across all subjects"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "Internally consistent: describes a monotonic decrease in intervention frequency tied to measurable improvement, with a clear termination condition",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "Suggestion-based change must require repeated reinforcement whose necessity genuinely declines with cumulative effect, and progress must be trackable in real time",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "The underlying process does not actually depend on renewed suggestions, improvement is not monotonically linked to interval lengthening, or 'cure' cannot be validly determined by discontinuation alone"
}

implicit_claims: Suggestions lose potency without periodic renewal

The same spacing rule applies across all subjects
